title: Tapiphyllum discolor
taxonomy: Tapiphyllum discolor is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae. It belongs to the genus Tapiphyllum and is native to New Guinea. The Lauraceae family consists of evergreen trees and shrubs, often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Tapiphyllum discolor is considered an endemic species, meaning it is only found in a specific geographic area. The classification of Tapiphyllum discolor reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
